The treatment -- it really isn't as bad as getting the tattoo -- the anticipation is worst.

review_text:
I recently discovered that I've developed an allergy to my own tattoo ink. It's been a long hard road, months of trying to figure out what's going on, and being referred from PCP, to Dermatology, to Allergist. Considering my situation, the thought of laser was especially not appealing. In fact, my Allergist warned that it may send me into shock, disbursing the ink throughout my body..

I had never even heard of T2A before all this (how??), but they have been so helpful. They scheduled a free consult, heard out my concerns, took notes of all my allergies, and even spoke to my Allergist to ensure they were being careful and considerate. They took their time and weren't just in a rush to get me on the books, taking my money even if it may not be good for me in my specific situation. You know?

Michelle has been great (everyone has). It was so good getting to meet her, the place was clean and organized, I got plenty of resource and information regarding care before/during/after, and she's been keeping in contact to make sure things are going well. I feel very fortunate to have even had this option so close to me.

Thank you for what you do. I'm sure you can't always guarantee how any given body will handle these things, but from my experience, I appreciate all of your time, attention to detail, and genuine care. Sincerely.
review_date_label: Jul 02, 2024 (verified from SerpAPI iso_date, last edited Jul 02, 2024)

review_text:
I do not recommend this company for tattoo removal. My first experience was on March 22nd and the technician, Maggie, was absolutely amazing. She took the time to explain the process to me and reassure me of any fears I had going in. She was very gentle, and pleasant and showed she cared not only about my tattoo horrible story, but the integrity of my skin. I had 2 services with her and each time was better than the last!
However, I’ve since tried to rebook Maggie and have sadly been informed that she is no longer employed there. I did receive treatment from one other technician since Maggie’s departure and I must say, my skin is swollen, painful and I fear I may walk away from this session with scars which was never a concern before. The woman that worked on me was not very nice, quick with the process and seemed like she could care less about the integrity of my skin and if I were to scar.
As a licensed esthetician in the service industry, I know a good worker when I see one and Maggie was that, the other lady, who I didn’t care to remember her name of, was not. I unfortunately will not be returning as I do worry about my skin health and I do not feel valued as a customer.

review_text:
***responding to their response…
It is simply not true that they have received no response from me. I text them on March 11th and after not hearing back, I sent a bubble “?”  To my text about scarring. After several days I received a text that I will have to come in and asked what time works best, which was just a few days ago. So the fact that they are saying they have made several attempts and haven't received a response is just simply not true.
Also them saying the pictures I attached are the ones showing irritations is also false. I didn’t even post those pictures original, I now have irritated photos posted. The pictures I posted are 2 months past my last appt. I have taken extremely good care of the wound (cause that is what it is) and the pictures are after all that extra care. That is the pictures of it healed! Which is why I text them about the scarring. You shouldn’t have to use tegaderm to not have scarring. This “tattoo removal” will scar you!

DO NOT get tattoo removal here! You will be left with a permanent scar where your tattoo was.

It’s appalling that this company is still operating. Their practices are not just unethical, but downright harmful. They are knowingly scarring their clients and should be shut down immediately! Avoid this company at all costs!

And for those that are thinking that I am just an angry customer and that there are enough good reviews to give it a try…I would encourage you to notice that none of the “good” reviews have before and after pictures. How would anyone give a good review for being permanently scarred??

These pics show the different stages. I have had 3 treatments and will never go back. This is the scar I am left with. 😖😔😡

review_text: I used this service to remove my eyebrow Microblading. I would not recommend it. I only went through with one of the two treatments, as the healing was so long, and I had vertical striped scabs that were very obvious. The striped scabs were to be expected, but the healing time was longer than expected. It also didn’t fully remove the ink in any of the strips that were treated. I also have slight scarring (pink, depressed skin). I started laser treatment today, and this seems like a much better and safer approach. I also contacted Tatt2away to ask for a partial refund since it didn’t remove my ink, and I wouldn’t be going through with the second prepaid treatment. I understand I signed a contract, but it would be good business practice and customer service to offer partial (half) refund.

review_text:
I can’t express enough how thrilled I am with my experience at Tat2Away, specifically with Mandy. From start to finish, Mandy exhibited the utmost professionalism, skill, and compassion, making my tattoo removal journey an absolute breeze.

First and foremost, Mandy’s expertise in tattoo removal is unparalleled. She took the time to thoroughly explain the process, answering all of my questions and alleviating any concerns I had. Her meticulous approach instilled confidence in me from the get-go.

Throughout each session, Mandy’s attention to detail was evident. She ensured that I was comfortable and informed every step of the way, making the process surprisingly comfortable. Her gentle demeanor and reassuring presence truly made a difference, especially during those initial sessions when I was a bit apprehensive.

Moreover, the results speak for themselves. Thanks to Mandy’s skillful technique and dedication, my tattoo has faded significantly with each session, exceeding my expectations. I’m beyond grateful for her expertise and the incredible results she has achieved.

Beyond her technical proficiency, Mandy’s empathy and kindness truly set her apart. She genuinely cares about her clients and goes above and beyond to make them feel valued and supported throughout the entire process. Her warmth and positivity made what could have been a daunting experience incredibly positive and empowering.

In conclusion, I wholeheartedly recommend Mandy at Tat2Away for anyone considering tattoo removal. Not only is she a master of her craft, but she’s also a wonderful person who genuinely cares about her clients’ well-being. Thank you, Mandy, for making this journey so rewarding. You’ve gained a lifelong fan in me!

If anyone is skeptical of this process or this company, please reach out to me personally, and I would be happy to give more detail
review_date_label: May 03, 2024 (verified from SerpAPI iso_date, last edited May 03, 2024)

review_text:
This company's technology looks amazing, and to its credit it may very well be a wonderful thing, HOWEVER, my personal experience with the clinic has convinced me that this is a very disorganized company, and furthermore one whose moral compass in terms of rational decision making is not skewed in a direction I admire.

After some initial phone tag, I was able to book an appointment for a first treatment with this clinic some weeks ago. However, because of the fact that I chose to be completely honest with my medical history at the time and disclosed that I believe I have a mild hypermobility syndrome (something I certainly could have kept secret if I’d known the trouble it would cause), my appointment was abruptly cancelled by the upper management due to (I would say extremely paranoid) concerns about liability issues. Please note, nothing on the website currently mentions that folks with a hypermobility disorder will be summarily rejected from treatment!!

Notably, I was not given any kind of say during this cancellation process. What irked me the most was I was not allowed to be part of any kind of conversation with management/the medical staff, despite the fact that this was a session that had been PROMISED to me and already BOOKED! And all this despite the fact that the tech who I had initially talked to said she was personally confident in and happy to give me the procedure despite the liability concerns, which she agreed were over the top given that I not only did not mind the idea of scarring, I actually thought it sounded cool were it to occur (I am a fan of the art form of scarification) and was willing to sign any kind of waiver to get the procedure done.

Finally, it was extremely puzzling and upsetting to me that my appointment was cancelled essentially behind my back and the only notification I got of the cancellation was an extremely vague text message saying “I spoke with our medical staff and just had a few things I wanted to touch base with you on.” Well, I had no idea what news was waiting in store for me, I figured the “few things” were just bureaucratic details, but I indeed tried calling back for several days in a row after this message. However, I was met with the answering machine each time (it seems like their phones are generally understaffed), and there was never any more personalized follow up from the clinic’s side. In other words, *I* had to chase THEM to finally get the confirmation that my appointment had indeed been cancelled since they didn’t have the courage(?)/common sense to tell me up front in the text they sent??? So, if I had not diligently followed up in this manner, I very well could have showed up to my appointment only to be told ON THE SPOT that my session had been cancelled without my knowledge or approval – and keep in mind, I am not living in Chicago full time right now so I was building my entire travel schedule around this one appointment.

Finally – and this is where it gets truly hilarious – weeks after the snafu with my cancelled appointment, I get a “follow up” text to an old message I had sent before scheduling the appointment saying “sorry this message got buried, when would you like to continue the conversation about booking?” I am no longer truly upset and can laugh about this now, but this company obviously needs to do some serious work in terms of client communications, PR, and staying on top of patient history.

Furthermore, it is unfortunately apparently so obsessed about liability issues that it can’t make a rational exception to take on a sane adult client in good faith (although I guess that’s the age we live in, thanks #america). It also seems to be totally insensitive to the psychological damage and upset it might be causing a prospective (and then later rejected) client by the snowball effect of such vague, contradictory, and misguided messages! Sorry, Tatt2Away, I wanted to love you - but come on, this is basic client rapport skills, 101!
review_date_label: May 02, 2024 (verified from SerpAPI iso_date, last edited May 02, 2024)

review_text:
Absolutely amazing team: straight forward intake and exciting first treatment.

Zac did amazing at making sure I understood both the procedure and science behind it. Felt really at ease to ask questions but didn’t have any as it was all very straightforward. He also did amazing at setting expectations based on the size and color of my tattoo.

review_text: I originally had a pink tattoo and after three sessions, it was about 95% gone. I came back in to get the last few areas, and the whole tattoo turned a green/blue. After another session, it got darker. I came back for another session and they assured me it would be gone after that. It has gotten darker with every session, and I just talked to another tattoo removal shop that told me it will be impossible to get out, because the white ink from the pink has oxidized and more sessions will continue to make it darker. They told me that whoever originally removed the tattoo should have known that and warned me about it. I'm incredibly disappointed that Clean Slate Ink did not responsibly remove my tattoo and inform me of what was going on, and continued to take my money while making the issue worse. I now have a green/blue faded tattoo on my wrist that looks worse than my original tattoo and can only be covered by an even larger tattoo. I'm honestly devastated. I would recommend going somewhere else that has more knowledge and experience and will be more up front with you.

review_text:
Posting this in hopes of saving someone a few dollars and to help manage expectations.

I started coming to cleanslate last September and in my consult was told that my tattoos should be gone within 6-8 sessions, and for sure ahead of my wedding in December of this year.

I have had 10 sessions now with little to no change at $250 a session. Once we hit the 8 session mark, I asked if they would be gone by my wedding. I was told, no, but they have really good makeup to cover them up.

I would not have put myself through the pain or investment if I wanted to find “good makeup”

I understand each person is unique but I feel the expectations and progress were severely mismanaged.

After receiving another opinion and being told that tattoo removal is always a 2 year process, I have decided to stop going to cleanslate.

I know a lot of their positive reviews are from 2-5 years ago so not sure if something has changed with equipment but thought this experience would be helpful to anyone considering this process.
_place_title: Clean Slate Ink - Tattoo Removal in Round Rock, TX

review_text:

review_text:
I’ve seen several different doctors/nurse injectors over the past couple years, and Clarity Skin takes the cake. One injector in specific, Emily, MUST HAVE SOLD HER SOUL TO THE DEVIL in exchange for the incredible ability she has. The injections she delivers are painless. In fact, I feel NOTHING. I seriously had no idea she had stuck a needle in me and when I asked about it, she said she was already done. I had to look in a mirror and see the injection site for myself to believe it. Perhaps her extensive experience being an OR nurse helped with that, as opposed to selling her soul to the devil.

Nah, it’s gotta be the devil thing.

P.S. the results are amazing. I waited a few months before reviewing just in case there were any surprises.

Edit: I went in again and my face got so messed up. I have TMJ and so Botox was recommended to weaken the masseter. At first, the TMJ pain subsided and I felt a noticeable difference. However, I soon *saw* a noticeable difference as well—and not a good one. The bottom half of my face got so thin and my jawline slackened. Should I have known Botox would have this effect? Yes, probably. However, I never even thought of it and was never told what the implications of receiving such treatment would entail, aesthetically speaking. I was embarrassed to go out into public. Also, I had happened to get a bad filler job around the same time, and for the first time, from them. I requested that filler be placed around and underneath my hollow eyes/dark circles, something I’ve had done many times before with amazing results. However, this time I came out looking like Maleficent and remained that way for months: high, rimmed orbital bones and pointy cheekbones, accompanied by a very slim face ending in a pointy chin.

Because I believe in good communication and in giving others a chance to try again, I went back. I told and showed them what was upsetting me and asked for help. It took several months to start building up my jaw muscles and then about two years to have it restored to normal. There was very little to be done except to wait out the Botox so the muscles could then rebuild themselves. They gave me a few hundred dollars off and generous samples of cosmetic and skin care products. I have waited years to edit this review so I could be further removed from this event and be as objective as possible in my feedback.
review_date_label: Nov 07, 2018 (verified from SerpAPI iso_date, last edited Aug 24, 2023)

review_text:
2025 - Still a loyal customer and today had a facial with Aysha!  I had one last month too and she did a full scan of my face - was an eye opener for sure, but that was really cool to see the damage (sad but cool).  Aysha created a plan to bring that youthful glow and undo (as much as possible) the damage over the years (I'm 60).  Today's facial was a bit different and longer than last month - she used some kind of mask (can't remember the name) and I immediately felt tightening of my face after.  Aysha is a true professional and is super knowledgeable.  Signed up for the monthly and can't wait for next month to see what magic she will do.  Oh, and the new location is right across the street from the old and it is beautiful!  Well done Loretta and Kristin (yes same name same spelling as me- automatic bond right there).  So happy to be a customer and so proud of the amazing business and caring team you have built!

2021 - Still going to Erasable Inc for my facials and other treatments.  They have specials all the time to help me save $$. They truly care about their customers and always go the extra mile. Luv ma gurls at EI!!
2020 - I have been going to erasable inc for over a year now.  Started out with tattoo removal (see review about that below) and then decided on other services such as their hydrafacial, botox (which I vowed I would never do...so glad I did), cool sculpting and true flex.  OMG the difference is amazing.  I work out and try to eat healthy most of the time (ice cream is a weakness) and even as a life long athlete, I still would have to work out 2 hours per day to get the results I got from cool sculpting and true flex.  The girls there are warm and welcoming, caring and considerate.  They have a clean healthy environment and are open for business but have done it thoughtfully and strategically for the safety of their patients.  I feel like I have a new family with the staff there.  Luv my EI girls!

2019 - Everyone at Eraseable Inc is incredibly knowledgeable and attentive.   I’m having a tattoo removed and can’t believe the difference from when I first came in.  Just a few more sessions to go and it’s already almost gone!   Got a great package price too that included multiple sessions and it saved me $ instead of paying for each visit.  They’ve got a great set up.   Next...cool sculpting!   Loretta and her team are very friendly and professional!    Thanks you guys!
review_date_label: Aug 23, 2018 (verified from SerpAPI iso_date, last edited Aug 22, 2025)

review_text:
I got laser hair removal here about 3 years ago or so. It’s been the best decision I’ve ever made. I haven’t had to think about shaving or waxing in forever and I wish I could’ve told my 18 year old self about it. I feel like a beautiful hairless cat. No more razor bumps or ingrown hairs. Honestly the cost seems high but if you think of the time and money you’re saving from traditional razors and stuff, it’s so worth it.

I had Diana as my person. She walked me through everything, was super professional and made me feel very comfortable from start to finish. She’s really got a way of making you feel calm and the entire experience was phenomenal.

I bought a package years ago, so I’m not sure what prices are like now. I still would’ve paid a pretty penny to not feel razor burn or any sort of pain from waxing. If you’re someone with dark hair and thus, dark armpits despite shaving, this helped tremendously.

The location had plenty of parking, the staff were very nice, and the place was really clean. I’ve already recommended Arviv to a few friends.
review_date_label: Mar 30, 2026 (verified from SerpAPI iso_date, last edited Mar 30, 2026)

review_text:
I went in as a new patient today… I showed up to
My appt on time.
I waited 45 mints yo be brought back, the nurse practitioner talked to me about the procedure l, her assistant came in, gave me a robe, a hair net, and placed topical anesthetic on the are to be laser, and I waited like an hour before I went to ask how much longer… office manager came in apologized and told me some excused about why I was waiting so long.
I told her I was going to wait but another 30 mts went by and the nurse never came.
I took off the robe and hair net and left the center without looking back.

My thoughts:… this practice of not telling the patient that there was a mistake on the schedule and move on to ask them to put on a robe and hairnet and placed topical anesthetic, so they have not choice but to wait in the room is a poor tactic business move that only infuriates people more.
Before that anesthetic was placed on my neck someone should had told me that they were running behind and asked me if I was willing to wait.
No one did that bc once a patient is on a robe and hairnet and anesthetized you then get them “stuck” waiting for the provider to come in:
That’s not a good business move… that only made me upset and come to Google to let people know that this center may not be as upfront and honest as they should be.
review_date_label: Mar 31, 2026 (verified from SerpAPI iso_date, last edited Mar 31, 2026)

review_text:
I kind of want to gatekeep, but this is too good not to share. Callie is so professional and amazing; I don’t trust anyone else to inject me!

She is very thorough and explains the entire tox process so I understand and really know what to expect. She’s very patient and doesn’t make me feel pressured to make any decisions on the spot. I really appreciate how she’s never pushy- she’s informative and will give me her honest opinion which is why I trust her so much. Callie does a great job of going over pricing before she gets any materials or equipment. The whole process is so seamless and she has little tricks and toys to help with my anxiety (I hate needles, though the pain is very minimal). She’s also follows up to ensure I love my results and if I’m not happy, she’ll give me suggestions to make sure I’m satisfied (though I’ve never not loved my results).

Also, Skintellect is a very friendly, clean, and safe environment. Everyone always has a smile on their face when I come in, and I’m greeted from the moment I open the door. The waiting area is very spacious and clean. I like how the establishment is very timely and I don’t have to wait long at all before I’m escorted to the next room. The staff members are kind and politely answer any questions I have, and give step by step details about how my appointment will go.

Overall, I’m highly satisfied and would recommend Callie and Skintellect to my friends and family.

review_text: I wanted to get laser hair removal and this was one the places that I checked. Out of all the other places I checked, (including the most well-known national brand for laser hair removal) they were the most affordable and the most flexible. While other places tried to pressure me into signing long term contracts and loans, Skintellect offered me the ability to get one treatment or do a package bundle for a couple of treatments. I picked them for my laser hair removal, because they offered me double the treatments as the other places at less than a 3rd of the price. While other places were pushy and kept hounding me with lower prices, discounts and limited time sales that never materialized, Skintellect from the very beginning offered me a straight forward affordable price and excellent treatment. From my very first treatment I saw drastic results and as I am nearing the end of my treatment, I am so glad I was able to find them. In a very crowded marketplace, they stand out among their competitors due to their excellent service, remarkable treatments and affordable prices. Shoutout to Kristina, who has worked with me when I am either early or late for my appointments (I am driving from Orlando, so traffic is difficult to predict), and has always been extremely knowledgeable, friendly and professional.

review_text:
I started removal for two of my tats at Removery about 2 years ago. I had already been in the removal process for about a year prior somewhere else. The laser Removery uses is SIGNIFICANTLY less painful than what I experienced at the place I was going before, which claimed to have had “the newest technology” (obviously bs). Removal is of course never going to be comfortable, but I would say the pain level with Removery’s laser is about a 4/10 compared to dang near close to a 10/10 at the other place.
I had some issues regarding a misleading consultation that resulted in unfair pricing, however, Angie the studio manager eagerly stepped in to help with a quick & fair solution. She is super sweet, along with all of the staff I’ve met at this location!
I’m still in the long & dreadful removal process unfortunately, but Removery is trying to get the job done as quickly as possible while also ensuring not to cause any additional skin damage I obtained at my prior removal place. I’m almost at the finish line, but super happy Removery prioritizes quality treatment over speed!
review_date_label: Mar 06, 2026 (verified from SerpAPI iso_date, last edited Mar 06, 2026)

review_text: The facility is clean the staff is great and the appointments are very quick. If you’re going to get black ink removed I’d highly recommend but as for color ink I would recommend not wasting your time or money. I’ve removed 5 black ink tattoos (only 1 ) with Removery and attempting to remove a purple ink tattoo but it’s not been successful. I had 7 sessions and all it did was change the color from purple to pink. Although they did warn there was NO guarantee I’m letting everyone know do NOT attempt or waste your money.

Update March 2026: I've faced some health struggles and the folks here have been unbelievably gracious about rescheduling, long breaks, and adjusting our treatment plan. review_text:
I am two weeks postop my FUE procedure with the folks at Enfuse and I couldn’t be happier with the evolving results & the process.

From the very beginning of my interest, Kevin was ready with any and all information, being very clear and realistic about what to expect for the procedure. He was very thoughtful and got ahead of many of my concerns, but did not sugarcoat or mislead me in any way about what to expect. Having gone through the procedure himself, he anticipated absolutely all of my needs & concerns before, during, and after the entire process.

FUE is a long-term investment and they all made it really clear what to expect over the next 12 months with the recovery time and long term follicle development. Dr Ioffe very clearly & concisely explained the process and assessed my health & medications while thoroughly reviewing my history.

During the procedure, Kevin and Dr. Ioffe worked gently & efficiently, keeping me informed of the progress while monitoring my well being. They & Patti were very conscious of asking if I needed breaks, how I was feeling, and assessing all of my needs.

For our lunch break, they ordered my favorite thing and set me up in a space privately, which was thoughtful & a nice break in a long day. They even made sure I was happy with whatever was playing on tv.

They prepared an aftercare kit that contained absolutely everything I needed for my recovery, many things I hadn’t considered. Again, it was so clear that Kevin had gone through this procedure himself and anticipated my every need. I followed their aftercare to the letter, and they’ve checked in often, confirming it has all gone according to plan.

I cannot stress enough, how safe and cared for I felt in there very professional, capable hands. I’m so grateful to have found these people.

review_text: When I first stepped into Enfuse I knew it was the place for me. I was greeted warmly and the space felt so comfortable and inviting. I fell in love with it. Taylor was amazing, she knew of my previous skin cancer and how nervous I was about treatments to my face. Taylor really took the time to analyze my skin and prepare a plan for me that would improve my skin and give me confidence and a beautiful glow. Enfuse really understands how all these treatments work together to give you the best possible results. Taylor started with the vascular laser which reduced my redness and eliminated broken capillaries. She also used IPL for my sun spots and sun damage. The real stars at Enfuse are the Frax and Morpheous8. Taylor did an incredible job explaining exactly what these processes do – resurfacing skin and improving tone, tightening texture and wrinkles. Specifically how even after the treatments are over they are still working for you for weeks to come, helping collagen production and new skin replacing old so you keep looking and feeling fresher and better. I cannot think of a more wonderful environment than Enfuse and to have someone as knowledgeable and specialized as Taylor helping me to navigate this journey. She is such a star, I can’t thank her enough and the team at Enfuse!!!

review_text:
I had such a great experience here that I felt like I had to share.

From the moment I walked in, Kevin at the front desk made me feel so welcomed and comfortable. He was kind, patient, and just had such a positive, calming energy—which honestly sets the tone for the whole visit.

Patti did my eyebrow tattoo laser removal, and I can’t say enough good things about her. I can finally say goodbye to my dark angry bird eyebrows!  She took her time to really listen to what I wanted to achieve with my face treatments and walked me through everything in a way that felt honest and thoughtful—not rushed or scripted. What stood out the most to me was that she didn’t try to push any products or upsell me. Instead, she genuinely explained what she would do if she were in my position trying to achieve the results I’m looking for. That kind of honesty is rare and so appreciated. As someone working hard to feel confident again and like what she sees in the mirror again, this felt like a breath of fresh air.

You can tell she truly cares about her clients and takes pride in her work. Her energy is warm, reassuring, and very genuine, which made me feel completely at ease throughout the entire process.

I also want to say—nowadays we rely so much on social media, and it’s easy to feel discouraged if you don’t see tons of before-and-after photos or videos of specific treatments. But this experience reminded me that it’s absolutely worth coming in for a consultation, because Patti is truly worth it. Her knowledge, honesty, and approach can’t always be captured online.

My commute was over an hour. Could I have found somewhere closer? Maybe. But finding someone with her kind of energy and positive aura made it more than worth the drive. I left feeling more confident and genuinely happy with my results—and to me, that’s worth the drive and then some.

I’m so happy I found this place, and I will 1,000% be coming back for my full face laser and Sculptra.

If you’re even thinking about booking, just do it. You’ll be in the best hands.

review_text:
I am blown away by my results!  I met with Patti for a consultation initially - which deserves it’s own moment.  It wasn’t a consult piggy-backed with a service but purely an offering to meet and discuss where I’m coming from and what outcomes I desire.  She genuinely listened and helped me design an approach with absolutely zero pressure.

My primary concern being my dark spots, Patti guided me toward the Light and Bright Treatment… my only regret is not doing this sooner!  I thought she was only planning on attacking the bigger spots; but she went after all noticeable hyperpigmentation!  It honestly took years off of my face!  Even the texture appears smoother, in addition to some bonus plumping Patti pointed out around the jowl area (from the collagen stimulation.)

The before/afters are stunning to me.
1 session.  3 weeks apart.
I could not be more thrilled!

I am so impressed and appreciative to Patti’s meticulousness, precision, and artist’s eye.  I can confidently say she is as passionate about your results, as you are.  Alongside all of this, she is just a joy to be around.  I stuck around at least 30 minutes after chatting with her and Kevin at the front desk.  Enfuse provides such a warm, welcoming environment and are incredibly supportive with follow-up communication/care/appmts.  I am so happy to know exactly where I'll be going for any skin/facial needs.  10/10 HIGHLY RECOMMEND!

review_text: I’ve been going to Enfuse for over a decade! My skin has completely transformed! I did something new a few weeks ago-  it was layering. I layered Frax, IPL Morpheus8 and Resolve. Patti told me it was a strategic way to target layers of the skin and different types of damage complete rejuvenation. I said, sign me up and rejuvenate my skin! I must admit I was a little scared to do so much work & that my face might look crazy after. But the best thing is that each treatment works in a different depth and on a different skin concern. And when you combine them, you can get even results with less overall downtime! So I was so excited to do it, and she said that they would numb my face and it wouldn’t be that bad at all! The IPL evened the color and tone of my skin and made it brighter! Then the Frax helped with my fine lines and some of the mild scarring I have on my face. It also promotes collagen stimulation! I need all of that! The Morpheus8, which is all the rage now, truly has tightened my skin I’ve done at least four of these in the last few years! Patti explained to me that the Morpheus8 lifts from underneath where the regular lasers can’t get to! The Resolve helped smooth my skin tone & especially my dark spots and old acne scars that I have from when I was younger! Overall, it was the best thing I ever did! I was able to go to work the next day! There was some redness etc but nothing too crazy! My skin looks amazing. It looks like I have make up on and I have nothing on! Don’t be scared. It’s 100% worth it! Truly, my skin has never looked better and it was so worth it! This is the best preventative care you can do for yourself. It’s the best medicine you can do for yourself!

review_text:
Enfuse is great and I'd highly recommend them! As a dude, I was a little nervous to go to a med spa, but they totally put you at ease (I'd only been to my dermatologist's office for laser hair removal before, never a med spa). All the staff is so friendly, from the person I called to book (who got me in that same day for a consult), to the consult staff, to the treatment staff. Truly just the nicest people that make you feel so comfortable and welcome. Love how they explain all the treatments to you, make recommendations, set realistic expectations. I was able to schedule quickly, the prices were VERY reasonable (I'm literally paying half the price I had been at my dermatologists office for laser hair removal), and they were able to suggest several treatments for enquiries I had about Morpheus8 and surgical scar reduction. I ended up booking several follow on treatments at my request, and really appreciated how they weren't pushing anything, it was all me asking what could be done about various scars and issues. Street parking also very easy right in front of their office, the space is really nice inside.

AND they offer Saturday and Sunday appointments instead of having to duck out of work during the week, which is so nice.

Highly recommend for anyone looking for a med spa, they've been in business for forever, and the team lead Patti now owns the place and was so helpful.

review_text:
For my 39th birthday, I gave myself a life upgrade and finally started removing an old tattoo I’ve wanted gone for years. From the moment I walked in, the space was clean, professional, and calming. The owner took the time to explain everything in detail, and I loved supporting a family-owned business. Shop local folks and support your small businesses!

They were incredibly flexible with my schedule, which I appreciated since I’m not in Houston often. I was able to start treatment the same day, had multiple pain management options, and the technician was so sweet and gentle and checked on me throughout the process.

This is my first tattoo removal experience, and I honestly couldn’t have asked for better. 10/10 so far. Pro tip: if you find them on Groupon, book the free consult first, they’ll help you choose the best option for your goals.

Added bonus:
One of the Owners voice sounds (at least to me) like the voice from ChatGBT and was super gracious when I demonstrated the comparison 😂

review_text:
If you're looking for a place that treats clients with care and empathy, then Inkfree is your spot. I didn’t end up going through with my removal, but I still wanted to leave this review in case it helps others make their decision.

When I arrived, Donna spent time with me before the procedure to answer all my questions and walk me through exactly what to expect so there were no surprises. While I was sitting in a separate room with numbing cream, I had time to process everything she shared and realized I wasn't fully ready to go through with the removal.

When I told them I wanted to cancel (even after everything was prepped and ready) they never pressured me or made me feel bad. They were calm, compassionate, and kept reminding me that it was okay to wait until I was 100% sure.

I hope this review helps anyone who might be nervous or unsure. Donna and the team here are incredibly kind and will treat you with care and patience, whether you go through with treatment or not.

Thank you for the kindness team. And when I’m ready, I’ll absolutely come back to see y’all.

Update

review_text: I cannot recommend this place enough, don’t hesitate to come here if you need permanent makeup removal. I came here to remove my eyebrows tattoo. I previously went thru 3 sessions at a different place, while my tattoo got lighter, it turned from brow to red and they couldn’t get rid of the red pigment. I almost gave up until i found this place. The staff are so friendly and knowledgeable about laser treatment. They explained to me the other place used the wrong machine/ wavelength, since eyebrow tattoo ink has many pigments, one machine can get rid of brown/ black but a different machine is required for red. My treatment was so quick, pain free and most importantly, they completely got rid of my tattoo. Their price is also very reasonable. I won’t go anywhere else but here if i need laser removal again

review_text:
Michelle at Tatt2Away Austin is a competent and kind technician with a great sense of humor! She has had 25 years of experience previously as an aesthetician, and brings her valuable insights to the process. She makes the process accessible and answers your questions without making you feel dumb, and adds a touch of humor where she can. Plus, she is so kind to make sure that your comfort is her top priority! I can say with confidence that you will be in great hands with her. :-) I highly recommend her!

Edit, Dec 2025:

I also have been treated by the new technician, Tatyana, who is very kind and knowledgeable about aesthetician work. She was trained by Michelle (who has been promoted) and she is excellent, now running this Austin location. Tatyana was thoughtful about my needs during the procedure and did it without wasting time and did it well.

You will be in great hands with her!

review_text:
I got a tattoo that I love but it's just bigger than what I would like for it to be. I went with Tatt2away bc of the more natural approach. Michelle is such a sweet, kind, and thoughtful person. She answered all my questions. I felt comfortable and opened up to her about my picking and scratching coping mechanism. She told me it would be a waste of money if I picked at the scabs and she wouldn't do the treatment if I felt like I wouldn't refrain from it. To have someone so honest means a lot to me.
The treatment -- it really isn't as bad as getting the tattoo -- the anticipation is worst.
I've done 1 treatment and it really just feels like a bad sunburn. I honestly thought it was going to look and feel a lot worst than what it is so far. I will keep y'all updated. 😊💗
Photos are initial, 24 hours after, and 48 hours after.

review_text:
This company's technology looks amazing, and to its credit it may very well be a wonderful thing, HOWEVER, my personal experience with the clinic has convinced me that this is a very disorganized company, and furthermore one whose moral compass in terms of rational decision making is not skewed in a direction I admire.

After some initial phone tag, I was able to book an appointment for a first treatment with this clinic some weeks ago. However, because of the fact that I chose to be completely honest with my medical history at the time and disclosed that I believe I have a mild hypermobility syndrome (something I certainly could have kept secret if I’d known the trouble it would cause), my appointment was abruptly cancelled by the upper management due to (I would say extremely paranoid) concerns about liability issues. Please note, nothing on the website currently mentions that folks with a hypermobility disorder will be summarily rejected from treatment!!

Notably, I was not given any kind of say during this cancellation process. What irked me the most was I was not allowed to be part of any kind of conversation with management/the medical staff, despite the fact that this was a session that had been PROMISED to me and already BOOKED! And all this despite the fact that the tech who I had initially talked to said she was personally confident in and happy to give me the procedure despite the liability concerns, which she agreed were over the top given that I not only did not mind the idea of scarring, I actually thought it sounded cool were it to occur (I am a fan of the art form of scarification) and was willing to sign any kind of waiver to get the procedure done.

Finally, it was extremely puzzling and upsetting to me that my appointment was cancelled essentially behind my back and the only notification I got of the cancellation was an extremely vague text message saying “I spoke with our medical staff and just had a few things I wanted to touch base with you on.” Well, I had no idea what news was waiting in store for me, I figured the “few things” were just bureaucratic details, but I indeed tried calling back for several days in a row after this message. However, I was met with the answering machine each time (it seems like their phones are generally understaffed), and there was never any more personalized follow up from the clinic’s side. In other words, *I* had to chase THEM to finally get the confirmation that my appointment had indeed been cancelled since they didn’t have the courage(?)/common sense to tell me up front in the text they sent??? So, if I had not diligently followed up in this manner, I very well could have showed up to my appointment only to be told ON THE SPOT that my session had been cancelled without my knowledge or approval – and keep in mind, I am not living in Chicago full time right now so I was building my entire travel schedule around this one appointment.

Finally – and this is where it gets truly hilarious – weeks after the snafu with my cancelled appointment, I get a “follow up” text to an old message I had sent before scheduling the appointment saying “sorry this message got buried, when would you like to continue the conversation about booking?” I am no longer truly upset and can laugh about this now, but this company obviously needs to do some serious work in terms of client communications, PR, and staying on top of patient history.

Furthermore, it is unfortunately apparently so obsessed about liability issues that it can’t make a rational exception to take on a sane adult client in good faith (although I guess that’s the age we live in, thanks #america). It also seems to be totally insensitive to the psychological damage and upset it might be causing a prospective (and then later rejected) client by the snowball effect of such vague, contradictory, and misguided messages! Sorry, Tatt2Away, I wanted to love you - but come on, this is basic client rapport skills, 101!
review_date_label: ~Apr 2025 (estimated from "a year ago", scraped April 24, 2026)

review_text: AVOID TATT2AWAY. My first and only session was Spring 2022. After proper aftercare and nearly four years later, the ink is mostly gone in the treated spots but I am also left with some hypertrophic scars from the procedure. If I were to continue with the removal process for the entire tattoo, I would choose laser or some other method, especially since its on my finger which has more sensitive and thinner skin more prone to scarring. Overall not impressed with the long-term results and wish I did more research before spending my money here. (The last photo was taken April 22, 2022, sometime after the procedure, the first two photos are fully healed December 2025)

review_text: I used this service to remove my eyebrow Microblading. I would not recommend it. I only went through with one of the two treatments, as the healing was so long, and I had vertical striped scabs that were very obvious. The striped scabs were to be expected, but the healing time was longer than expected. It also didn’t fully remove the ink in any of the strips that were treated. I also have slight scarring (pink, depressed skin). I started laser treatment today, and this seems like a much better and safer approach. I also contacted Tatt2away to ask for a partial refund since it didn’t remove my ink, and I wouldn’t be going through with the second prepaid treatment. I understand I signed a contract, but it would be good business practice and customer service to offer partial (half) refund.

review_text: Tatt2away completely mutilated by 18 year olds arm. She went back to the clinic and they said she was having a “reaction”. After several visits with the dermatologist the doctor confirmed that it wasn’t a reaction, it was a completely botched tattoo removal that has left her with extreme keloid scars on her whole arm. The staff did not try to help her or offer her a refund or anything! Just a completely unconcerned well, your body is reacting. What an inhumane response to hurting such a young one so badly. She is undergoing medical treatment from the damage they caused but she will ultimately be scarred for life. For anyone considering this clinic I would stay well away. The damage they caused is severe and their unconcerned attitude regarding how badly they hurt her and botched the job shows TERRIBLE ethics. This photo is months post treatment. She will have these hard raised scars for life. It also seems like the business is flagging and removing negative reviews as this review is no longer publicly available. Wow.
